title: Exploiting Yul right-to-left function argument evaluation
solidity_before:
bool success = token.staticcall(...);
bool valid = success && data.length >= 32;
explanation: In Yul, function arguments are evaluated from right to left. By placing the `staticcall` as the rightmost argument inside an `and()` check, the EVM executes the external call first. Once the call completes, the left argument `gt(returndatasize(), 0x1f)` evaluates the size of the return data resulting from that exact call. This collapses multiple assignments and variable allocations into a single, highly efficient stack operation.
when_to_apply: Chaining execution state outcomes (like `call` success) with subsequent post-state checks (like `returndatasize()`).
when_not_to_apply: When the right-side evaluation modifies state that the left-side evaluation relies on in a complex or unpredictable way.
